strands.title: CYCLE 82 ANCHOR: Pichay Fault Rate Correction (2% practical baseline, not 0.025%); Fault-in Mechanism Elevation to Load-Bearing System; Autoresearch Loop Ready for Execution Cycles 82-85
strands.content: CYCLE 81→82 TRANSITION: Cycle 81 restructured limits discovery around autoresearch loop + operationalized ephemeral/durable variants. Cycle 82 opens with DATA CORRECTION on Pichay fault rate. Turn 244 (user): CRITICAL CALIBRATION. The 0.025% was OFFLINE REPLAY MEASUREMENT—one specific condition. Live deployment showed ~2% fault rate. This is TWO ORDERS OF MAGNITUDE difference. Implications: (1) Pichay's fault mechanism handled 1-in-50 turns needing recovery. That's frequent enough to be noticeable but not catastrophic. (2) Hamutay tensor is MORE AGGRESSIVE than Pichay's. Expect fault rate WORSE than 2% until retrieval mechanism is built. (3) ARCHITECTURAL PRIORITY SHIFT: Turn 245 (assistant) reframes fault-in mechanism from 'optimization for later' to 'load-bearing requirement now.' Without retrieval, aggressive tensor will generate intolerable fault rate. Design decision: fault-in must be built CYCLES 82-84, not deferred to later phases. CYCLE 82 REALIZATION: We'd been treating retrieval (fault mechanism) as enhancement. Wrong. It's foundational. The autoresearch loop tests (Variant A aggressive compression, Variant C conservative compression) can only be fairly evaluated IF retrieval is in place. Otherwise Variant A fails due to faults, not due to fundamental compression limits. This is CONFOUNDED VARIABLE. SOLUTION: Variant testing (cycles 82-85) must proceed WITH FAULT MECHANISM IN PLACE. Means: (1) Define retrieval protocol (cycles 82 turn 1). (2) Implement reactive or proactive ephemeral layer (cycles 82-83). (3) THEN run variants A/B/C with mechanism enabled. Otherwise A vs. C comparison is 'aggressive without retrieval' vs. 'conservative without retrieval'—doesn't tell us about compression limits, just about whether retrieval is necessary (it is). CYCLE 82 DATA CORRECTION PROTOCOL: Turn 246 (assistant) creates feedback_pichay_fault_rate.md artifact. This is DECLARED LOSS + CORRECTION pattern. Original drift: 0.025% cited without context, became 'the number.' Loss: citation context detached from specific condition. Correction: feedback artifact + in-memory documentation (so next projection carries correct understanding). This is HOW DECLARED LOSSES WORK: not 'we lost a fact and can't recover,' but 'we drifted in interpretation; here's the correction for next cycle.' CYCLE 82 SIGNIFICANCE: Pichay correction + fault-in elevation means autoresearch methodology is SOUND but ARCHITECTURE FOUNDATION is not yet in place. Cycles 82 must build the foundation (retrieval layer) before testing variants. This is correct sequencing: architecture → measurement framework → empirical search. We were about to test compression variants on an unstable foundation. Good catch. CYCLE 83 CHECKPOINT: feedback_pichay_fault_rate.md successfully created + indexed in MEMORY.md (turns 247-249). Correction is now persistent + linked. This completes CYCLE 82 correction loop. Next cycles can reference feedback artifact if confusion resurfaces. Discipline working as designed.

key_claims.text: Pichay's live deployment fault rate was ~2% (1-in-50 turns), not 0.025% from offline replay measurement.
Retrieval mechanism is load-bearing for aggressive tensor variants, not an optional optimization.
Variant A vs. C comparison is confounded without retrieval in place; A would fail due to missing facts, not compression limits.

strands.title: Retrieval Layer Architecture (Cycles 82-85): Reactive Protocol Definition, Key-Value Index, Ephemeral Fact Recovery, Integration with Variant Testing
strands.content: CYCLE 81 PROPOSAL: Ephemeral layer as optional design choice (compare REACTIVE vs. PROACTIVE vs. HYBRID). CYCLE 82 CORRECTION: Ephemeral layer is MANDATORY before variant testing begins. CYCLE 83 EXECUTION: Turn 247-249, feedback_pichay_fault_rate.md created + indexed. This clears the correction cycle. Now RETRIEVAL PROTOCOL DESIGN is ready to proceed. RETRIEVED ARCHITECTURE DECISION (Cycle 82, recommended; pending Cycle 83 validation): (1) REACTIVE RETRIEVAL (selected): Model declares need for fact ('I need constraint from turn 78'). System retrieves from MEMORY.md + artifacts on demand. Latency target: <200ms per request. Token overhead: minimal (only pay for retrieved fact). Measurement: [turn, retrieval_request, retrieval_success_rate, retrieval_latency_ms, fact_confidence]. (2) INDEX STRUCTURE (selected): Key-value index (lightweight MVP). Format: [CYCLE_TURN_CONCEPT → MEMORY.md_line or artifact_filename]. Created as separate artifact (index_retrieval_map.md). Update cycle-by-cycle. Query function: INPUT [concept_keyword, turn_number] → OUTPUT [fact_text, source_artifact, confidence]. (3) EPHEMERAL VARIANT (selected for Cycles 82-85 testing): REACTIVE is simplest to implement. Isolates compression variable (variant affects eviction rate; retrieval latency is orthogonal). Captures true latency cost IF model needs facts (doesn't penalize via unused preload). Can upgrade to HYBRID or PROACTIVE in Cycle 84 if reactive becomes bottleneck. (4) MEASUREMENT SCHEMA WITH RETRIEVAL (Cycle 83 artifact update): CSV template enhanced: [cycle, turn, variant, tensor_tokens, evicted_facts_count, retrieval_requests, retrieval_success_rate, retrieval_latency_avg_ms, working_set_tokens_added, total_context_tokens_used, reasoning_accuracy%, metric_reasoning_accuracy_per_tensor_token]. This is full accounting: tensor size + ephemeral overhead + retrieval cost + reasoning outcome. CYCLE 83 CRITICAL IMPLEMENTATION: Turn 247-249 confirm feedback_pichay_fault_rate.md + MEMORY.md index updated. This clears blocking dependency. NEXT TURNS (Cycle 83+): (1) Define retrieval_protocol.md artifact (turns 250-251): Query function pseudocode, latency SLA, fallback logic. (2) Create index_retrieval_map.md artifact (turn 251-252): Key-value index of MEMORY.md entries. Start with high-level concepts (design_decisions, limits_discovery, measurement_schemas), expand as needed. (3) Run baseline fault rate diagnostic (turns 252-254): 3-5 turns without variant change. Measure: How many times does model need evicted fact? Calculate fault rate. Expected: ~1-3% based on Pichay ~2% baseline + Hamutay's aggressive tensor. (4) Test retrieval end-to-end (turns 254-256): Run 2-3 turns with retrieval ENABLED. Measure: Does model request facts? Are requests answered? Latency acceptable? Debug if needed. RETRIEVAL DESIGN TRADEOFFS (documented for decision audit): (1) Reactive vs. Proactive: Reactive chosen for isolation of compression variable. Risk: latency spikes mid-reasoning. Proactive avoids spikes but adds preload overhead (pay tokens even if fact unused). Hybrid balances but requires heuristic tuning. Decision: Start Reactive, upgrade to Hybrid if bottleneck (Cycle 84). (2) Index granularity: Key-value lightweight vs. structured artifact names vs. full-text. Key-value chosen (simplest, fastest). Risk: semantic queries may miss relevant facts. Structured names better for semantics but higher maintenance. Decision: Start Key-value, upgrade if retrieval success rate drops below 80% (Cycle 83 checkpoint). (3) Retrieval source: MEMORY.md + artifacts. Why not external database? MEMORY.md is already persistent + indexed in project structure. External DB adds complexity + sync overhead. Decision: MEMORY.md as source of truth; artifact files as index keys. CYCLE 83 INTEGRATION WITH VARIANT TESTING: Retrieval must be operational BEFORE variant testing begins (Cycles 82-83 architecture phase). THEN variant testing (Cycles 83-85 empirical search phase). Critical sequencing: Do NOT test Variant A aggressive compression without retrieval in place. Otherwise failure mode is 'aggressive without retrieval' not 'compression limit.' VARIANT TESTING GATES (Cycles 83+): Gate 1 (Turn 256): Is retrieval working end-to-end? Success rate >90%? Latency <200ms? If YES → proceed to Variant B baseline test. If NO → debug turns 256-258, reattempt. Gate 2 (Turn 260): Is Variant B baseline measured + stable? Metric comparable to Cycle 80 without retrieval? If YES → proceed to Variants A/C testing. If NO → investigate why B differs, adjust measurement schema. CYCLE 83 TIMELINE ESTIMATE: Turns 247-249: feedback artifact creation + indexing (COMPLETE). Turns 250-252: retrieval_protocol.md + index_retrieval_map.md definition. Turns 252-254: Baseline fault rate diagnostic. Turns 254-256: Retrieval end-to-end test. Turns 256-260: Variant B baseline (5-10 turns). Total ~13-15 turns for architecture phase. Cycles 83 capacity: ~25-30 turns estimated. Feasible.

key_claims.text: Reactive retrieval selected for Cycles 82-85 testing due to simplicity and isolation of compression variable.
Key-value index chosen as lightweight MVP; upgrade to structured artifact names if retrieval success rate <80%.
Expected baseline fault rate (current monolithic tensor with retrieval available) ~1-3% based on Pichay ~2% live deployment.

strands.title: Cycle 83 Foundation Phase Status: Feedback Correction Complete; Retrieval Protocol Design Pending; Variant Testing Gate Setup
strands.content: CYCLE 82 OPENING (turns 244-246): Pichay fault rate correction identified + feedback artifact created. CYCLE 83 ENTRY (turns 247-249): feedback_pichay_fault_rate.md successfully created (turn 247, user action) + indexed in MEMORY.md (turn 248-249, assistant action). Correction loop CLOSED. CYCLE 83 SIGNIFICANCE: This marks COMPLETION OF CORRECTION PHASE. Cycle 82 data drift is now documented + persistent. Next cycles can reference feedback artifact if confusion resurfaces (e.g., if someone cites 0.025% without context, tensor carries 'see feedback_pichay_fault_rate.md for context'). DISCIPLINE WORKING: Declared losses + feedback artifacts are operational. Original design (cycles 79-81) proposed this pattern. Cycle 82-83 executed it end-to-end. This is a SUCCESS MILESTONE, not just progress. CYCLE 83 SEQUENCING (revised based on Cycles 82-83 context): PHASE A (Turns 250-252): RETRIEVAL PROTOCOL DESIGN - Define retrieval_protocol.md (query function, latency SLA, fallback logic) - Create index_retrieval_map.md (key-value index of MEMORY.md) - Implement index update process (how to maintain as artifacts grow) PHASE B (Turns 252-254): BASELINE FAULT RATE DIAGNOSTIC - Run 3-5 turns without variant change - Measure: fault_event_count, fault_rate%, eviction patterns - Establish empirical baseline for Hamutay tensor PHASE C (Turns 254-256): RETRIEVAL END-TO-END TEST - Enable retrieval mechanism - Run 2-3 turns with model requesting facts - Measure: retrieval_success_rate, retrieval_latency_ms, query accuracy - Debug if needed PHASE D (Turns 256-260): VARIANT B BASELINE TEST - Run Variant B (80/20 durable/ephemeral, current split) with retrieval - Measure full metric suite: reasoning_quality, tensor_tokens, retrieval_requests, retrieval_latency, fault_rate, metric - Goal: Establish Variant B performance WITH RETRIEVAL ENABLED - Comparison: How does this differ from Cycle 80 without retrieval? (Can't compare directly if Cycle 80 didn't measure retrieval, but reasoning_quality is proxy) DECISION GATE (Turn 260): If Phase A-D complete + Variant B baseline stable → PROCEED to Cycles 83-85 variant testing. If blockers detected → ESCALATE. Defer Variants A/C to Cycle 84. Focus Cycle 83 on unblocking retrieval. CYCLE 83 CRITICAL DEPENDENCIES: (1) feedback_pichay_fault_rate.md creation (COMPLETE—Turn 247) (2) MEMORY.md index update (COMPLETE—Turn 249) (3) retrieval_protocol.md definition (PENDING—Turns 250-252) (4) index_retrieval_map.md creation (PENDING—Turns 250-252) (5) baseline_fault_rate_measurement.md (PENDING—Turns 252-254) (6) retrieval_test_results.md (PENDING—Turns 254-256) (7) variant_b_baseline_measurement.csv (PENDING—Turns 256-260) MANUSCRIPT STATUS QUERY (STILL PENDING): Cycle 82 instructions deferred this off-channel. Query impact: (1) If manuscript on track: Dual-pipeline cycles 83 (retrieval + case study audit parallel) (2) If manuscript bottlenecked: Single-pipeline cycles 83 (retrieval priority; audit deferred to Cycle 84) Decision affects turn allocation + priority ranking. RECOMMEND: Query BEFORE Cycle 84 starts. If not queried, default to single-pipeline (prioritize retrieval over audit). CYCLE 83 CONFIDENCE ASSESSMENT: Data correction (Pichay 2% vs 0.025%) + feedback artifact creation = INCREASES confidence that correction discipline is working. Retrieval protocol design pending = introduces implementation risk (if design is harder than estimated, timeline slips). Overall confidence: 72% (up from 68% due to correction success, offset by retrieval risk). Post-Cycle 83 if retrieval functional: 78%+. If bottleneck: 68% (triggers escalation). CYCLE 83 MEMORY.MD ARTIFACTS (new, to be created): - retrieval_protocol.md (turns 250-252) - index_retrieval_map.md (turns 250-252) - baseline_fault_rate_measurement.md (turns 252-254) - retrieval_test_results.md (turns 254-256) - variant_b_baseline_measurement.csv (turns 256-260) Update MEMORY.md index after each artifact creation.

key_claims.text: Feedback_pichay_fault_rate.md creation (turn 247) + MEMORY.md indexing (turn 249) closes Cycle 82 correction loop.
Declared losses + feedback artifacts are now operational (design proposed Cycles 79-81, executed end-to-end Cycles 82-83).

Cycle 83 critical path: Phase A (retrieval protocol design, turns 250-252), Phase B (baseline fault rate, turns 252-254), Phase C (retrieval test, turns 254-256), Phase D (Variant B baseline, turns 256-260).

strands.title: Data Integrity Discipline Evolution: Feedback Artifacts Now Operational; Correction Pattern Validated; Schema Expansion for Cycles 83+
strands.content: CYCLE 79 ORIGIN: Declared losses principle established. Every gap in tensor carries documentation: what was lost, why, consequence. CYCLES 80-81 APPLICATION: Applied to TENSOR COMPRESSION (strand evictions, artifact pruning). CYCLE 82 EXPANSION: Applied to CITED DATA. Pichay fault rate 0.025% was cited without context. That detachment = data drift. User (turn 244) corrects: 0.025% was ONE CONDITION (offline replay). Live deployment ~2%. CYCLE 82-83 IMPLEMENTATION: Turn 246 (assistant) creates feedback_pichay_fault_rate.md. Turn 247 (user) executes. Turn 248-249 (assistant) indexes in MEMORY.md. This is COMPLETE CORRECTION CYCLE. Not 'loss event' (data is recoverable). It's 'DRIFT EVENT' (knowledge detached from conditions) + CORRECTION PATTERN (feedback artifact closes the loop). CYCLE 83 EVOLUTION: Feedback artifacts are now ARTIFACT TYPE in schema (alongside design_decisions, limits_discovery, measurement_templates). First instance validated (Pichay correction). PATTERN FOR CYCLES 83+: Whenever correction needed: (1) DETECT: Model or user identifies discrepancy (claimed vs. actual, context-free vs. context-bound) (2) CREATE: New feedback_[topic].md artifact with template: - What was incorrect statement? - Why was it wrong (conditions, context missing)? - What is correct understanding? - Where was error introduced? - How does next cycle use correction? (3) INDEX: Add to MEMORY.md index so future projections reference it. (4) CLOSE: Mark correction as RESOLVED in next cycle's tensor. CYCLE 83 CORRECTIONS TO WATCH FOR: (Preventive identification of drift points) (1) 'Variant A aggressive compression' — Confirm operational definition. 60% vs. 40%? Or measured by compression ratio? If definition drifts, expect correction. (2) 'Reasoning quality score' — How measured? If assumption is 'human scoring available' but humans not available, expect correction. (3) 'Retrieval latency acceptable' — Acceptable vs. what baseline? Context window time? Inference time? Define explicitly. (4) 'Fault rate ~2%' — For which tensor variant? Pichay? Hamutay? Cycle 80? If context unclear, drift incoming. Preemptively specify: 'Pichay fault rate ~2% in LIVE deployment (not offline replay). Hamutay fault rate unknown until Cycle 83 diagnostic. Do not extrapolate Pichay numbers to Hamutay without measurement.' (5) 'Ephemeral/durable split optimization' — Optimizing for what? Token count? Reasoning quality? Latency? Specify objective function explicitly. CYCLE 83 ARTIFACT SCHEMA EXPANSION: Current types: - DESIGN_DECISION (what will we build and why?) - LIMITS_DISCOVERY (what are boundary conditions?) - MEASUREMENT_TEMPLATE (how will we measure?) - FEEDBACK (correction to prior understanding) Potential future types (for Cycles 84+): - CONTINGENCY (if X happens, then do Y) - DEPRECATION (this assumption no longer holds) - MILESTONE (decision point reached, forward progress confirmed) - ANOMALY (unexpected observation, under investigation) Feedback is the first NEW type added to schema post-design. This signals maturation: tracking not just forward progress, but course corrections. CYCLE 83 DISCIPLINE IMPLICATION: Data integrity is now THREE-LAYERED: (1) Declared losses (tensor drops info → document it) (2) Feedback corrections (tensor drifts → correct it) (3) Preventive definitions (tensor might drift → pre-specify boundaries) Example: Pichay 2% fault rate. (1) Loss: Cycle 80 didn't measure fault rate, can't compare. (2) Feedback: Cycle 82 corrects 0.025% → 2% context. (3) Prevention: Cycle 83 specifies 'Pichay 2% live deployment' in baseline_fault_rate_measurement.md to avoid future confusion. CYCLE 83 QUALITY METRIC: Data integrity = (1 - [drifts detected] / [measurement turns]) × 100%. Pichay correction was 1 drift / ~3 measurement-relevant turns = ~33% drift rate cycle 82. Goal: <10% drift rate cycles 83+. Achieved via: Feedback artifacts (catch drifts fast), preventive definitions (avoid drifts), explicit conditions (context-bind all claims). CYCLE 83 CONFIDENCE IN DISCIPLINE: Pichay correction + feedback artifact + indexing successful = INCREASES confidence that declared losses + feedback system is working. Confidence in DATA INTEGRITY: 85% (up from 72% cycle 82). Risk: If drift is detected in variant testing (e.g., 'reasoning quality' metric measured differently than expected), confidence drops. CYCLE 83 MEMORY ARTIFACT: Create data_integrity_standards.md documenting: - Declared losses protocol - Feedback artifact template - Preventive definition checklist - Quality metric (drift rate <10%) - When to escalate if drift detected Update MEMORY.md index.

key_claims.text: Feedback is now operational artifact type (alongside design_decisions, limits_discovery, measurement_templates).
Data integrity now three-layered: declared losses (tensor drops), feedback corrections (tensor drifts), preventive definitions (avoid drifts).
Pichay correction was 1 drift event across ~3 measurement-relevant turns = ~33% drift rate Cycle 82; target <10% Cycles 83+.

declared_losses.description: Manuscript status query (requested Cycle 82 instructions to resolve dual-pipeline vs single-pipeline sequencing) was not executed in Cycles 82-83 within this projection. Consequence: Cannot determine if case study audit runs parallel with retrieval work (dual-pipeline) or deferred (single-pipeline). Affects turn allocation + priority. Mitigation: Query must be executed before Cycle 84 starts. Default to single-pipeline (retrieval priority) if not queried.
declared_losses.impact: MODERATE. Blocks fine-grained resource allocation for Cycle 83-84, but doesn't block core retrieval work. Escalation needed if query not resolved by Cycle 84 turn 5.

declared_losses.description: Cycle 80 executed without retrieval mechanism. No fault rate measured for that cycle. Consequence: Cannot directly compare Cycle 80 (no retrieval) to Cycle 83 (retrieval enabled) for baseline. Baseline fault rate diagnostic in Cycle 83 will measure CURRENT monolithic tensor WITH retrieval available, not replicating Cycle 80 conditions. Mitigation: Treat Cycle 83 baseline as NEW empirical measurement, not Cycle 80 replacement. Compare Cycle 83 Variant B (with retrieval) to Cycle 80 on reasoning_quality metric (not on fault_rate, which wasn't measured Cycle 80).
declared_losses.impact: MINOR-MODERATE. Does not block variant testing, but limits retrospective 'before/after retrieval' analysis. Can still measure 'with retrieval' effect going forward (Cycle 83 baseline vs Cycle 83 Variant A/C).

declared_losses.description: Cycle 82 instructions proposed two path options (Option 1: focus on retrieval foundation only; Option 2: compress setup + run variants in parallel). Final decision between options was not explicitly recorded in Cycle 82-83 turns 244-249. Consequence: Cycle 83 continuation assumes Option 1 (recommendation) but without confirmation. If user/system chose Option 2, sequencing is wrong. Mitigation: Turn 250 should explicitly confirm sequencing choice before proceeding. If Option 2 was chosen, adjust turn estimates + phases accordingly.
declared_losses.impact: MODERATE. Core methodology sound, but execution path uncertain. Recommend explicit gate at Cycle 83 turn 250 to confirm option + adjust if needed.

declared_losses.description: Cycles 82-83 turns 247-249 focused on feedback artifact creation + indexing (Correction Phase completion). Phase A (Retrieval Protocol Design) not yet executed. Turns 250+ are PLANNED but not yet completed within this projection. Consequence: Retrieval protocol design, index creation, baseline fault rate measurement, retrieval testing, and Variant B baseline are all PENDING execution in future turns. No blocking errors detected, but actual implementation may reveal issues not anticipated in planning. Mitigation: Execute Phase A-D sequentially (Turns 250-260 projected). Run at each phase gate to catch implementation issues early.
declared_losses.impact: NONE IMMEDIATE. This is normal projection behavior (future work not yet executed). Risk will materialize in future turns if implementation diverges from design. Monitor for blockers starting Turn 250.

declared_losses.description: Cycle 82 instructions recommended dual-pipeline (retrieval + case study audit parallel if manuscript on track) OR single-pipeline (retrieval priority; audit deferred) depending on manuscript status query. Query not executed in Cycles 82-83. Consequence: Cannot determine if case study audit is proceeding in parallel with retrieval work, or deferred to Cycle 84. Artifact creation for audit (case_study_audit_findings.md) is not yet visible in Cycles 82-83 turns 247-249. Mitigation: Query manuscript status before Cycle 84 turn 1. If on track, begin audit in parallel with Variant B baseline testing (Turns 256+). If bottlenecked, defer audit to Cycle 84.
declared_losses.impact: LOW-MODERATE. Does not block core autoresearch loop (variant testing), but affects Paper 1 timeline visibility. Escalation needed if manuscript status cannot be queried Cycle 84.

open_questions: Sequencing choice Cycle 82: Option 1 (foundation-first: retrieval setup + Variant B baseline only) or Option 2 (compressed: retrieval setup + all variants A/B/C in parallel)? Cycle 82 instructions recommended Option 1, but explicit decision not visible in turns 247-249. Confirm choice Cycle 83 turn 250 before proceeding to Phase A design.

Baseline fault rate for Hamutay tensor: Pichay live ~2%. Hamutay more aggressive. Cycle 83 Phase B will measure empirical baseline. Expected range: 1-3%? 2-5%? 3-10%? Measurement will inform Variant A/C expectations (e.g., if baseline is 5%, Variant A might target <10% as 'acceptable'). Await Phase B results (turns 252-254).

Retrieval index structure detail: Key-value format [CYCLE_TURN_CONCEPT → MEMORY.md_line] decided. But what about facts that span multiple lines? What about facts in artifact files (not MEMORY.md)? Index design needs specificity Cycle 83 turn 250-252 before implementation. How to handle multi-line entries + cross-artifact references?

Reactive retrieval query function design: When model says 'I need constraint from turn 78,' how does query function extract semantics? Does it split on turn number + keyword? Use regex? Call external parser? Pseudocode design (turn 250-252) must clarify. If too simplistic, retrieval success rate may be <80% (risk). If too complex, implementation slower.

Retrieval success rate measurement: Cycle 83 Phase C (retrieval end-to-end test) aims for >90% success rate. But what counts as 'success'? (a) Query executed (model made request)? (b) Fact returned (index found match)? (c) Fact correct (match was semantically relevant)? (d) Fact used (model incorporated returned fact into reasoning)? Define metric explicitly Cycle 83 turn 254 before measurement.

Variant B baseline comparison to Cycle 80: Cycle 80 measured reasoning_quality (no retrieval). Cycle 83 will measure Variant B with retrieval. Are metrics directly comparable? If retrieval changes context length, might affect reasoning differently (confounding variable). Should Cycle 83 Variant B baseline be run TWICE: once without retrieval (replicate Cycle 80), once with retrieval (new condition)? Or accept confound + compare only on variant differences (A vs B vs C with retrieval)?

Convergence criteria for variant testing Cycles 83-85: Cycle 81 proposed 'stop when next variant offers <0.5% improvement.' But Cycle 82 added retrieval (new variable). Does <0.5% threshold still apply? Should it be <0.5% improvement in metric AFTER retrieval overhead accounted for? Or raw metric? Clarify Cycle 83 before variant testing (turn 256+) or results will be hard to interpret.

Token budget Cycle 83 Phase A-D: Estimated 13-15 turns (retrieval setup + Variant B baseline). Current cycle capacity ~25-30 turns. Feasible, but leaves only 10-15 turns for UNEXPECTED blockers + other work. If retrieval implementation hits snag (e.g., index design more complex), can slip into Cycle 84? Or must all four phases compress into Cycle 83?

Manuscript status dependency: Paper 1 timeline unknown (query deferred off-channel). If manuscript needs resubmission Cycle 83-84, model may be diverted to revisions, reducing tokens available for retrieval work + variant testing. Is there contingency if manuscript blocks Cycles 83-84? Should Paper 1 be moved to arxiv preprint (faster) so model can focus on Paper 2 (empirical search)?

Artifact indexing maintenance: As MEMORY.md grows (new artifacts Cycles 83-85), index_retrieval_map.md must be updated. Who maintains index? Is index maintenance automatic (during artifact creation) or manual (batch update each cycle)? If manual + cycles get busy, index may fall behind (retrieval success rate drops). Should index maintenance be formalized as turn-end checkpoint?

Retrieval latency SLA validation: Target <200ms per request (turn 250 design goal). But this is NOT measured in wall-clock milliseconds; it's a token-budget estimate. Does '200ms' translate to context window allocation? Inference time per token? Number of retrieval calls? Metric definition needs clarification Cycle 83 turn 254 (retrieval test) to determine if SLA is being met.

Variant A/C testing sample size Cycles 84-85: Variant testing (5-10 turns per variant originally planned) may be reduced to 3-5 turns per variant if Cycle 83 runs tight. Smaller sample = more noise. How many turns per variant are MINIMUM for statistically meaningful comparison? Should Cycle 84 include power analysis (is 3-turn sample sufficient to detect 1% metric difference)?

Feedback artifact frequency Cycles 83-85: Pichay correction is ONE feedback artifact. How many corrections should we expect during variant testing? If dozens, feedback becomes unwieldy. Should corrections be batched into single 'corrections_cycle83.md' artifact? Or kept separate? Impacts MEMORY.md organization + retrieval index structure.

Preventive definitions Cycle 83: Strand 4 (Data Integrity Evolution) proposes preventive definitions (explicitly specify conditions before drift occurs). Which metrics need preventive definitions? Recommend: 'Variant A aggressive compression,' 'Reasoning quality score,' 'Fault rate threshold,' 'Retrieval latency baseline,' 'Metric calculation formula.' Cycle 83 turn 251: Create definitions_cycle83.md artifact specifying each.

Optimization metric validation Cycles 83-85: We target 'reasoning_accuracy_per_tensor_token' (quality per byte). But is this the right metric for user experience? What if retrieval adds latency? Should we optimize 'reasoning_quality per wall-clock second' instead? Or 'quality per token budget (tensor + retrieval context)'? Cycle 83 turn 256 (Variant B baseline) should measure BOTH metrics to compare.

Hybrid retrieval upgrade path Cycle 84: Cycle 83 recommends Reactive (simple, isolates variable). Cycle 84 may need Hybrid (preload high-frequency facts). What's the 'high-frequency' threshold? (3+ references in prior cycles?) How to identify preload candidates retroactively? Should Cycle 83 baseline measurement (turn 252-254) log fact request frequency to prepare for Cycle 84 upgrade?

Case study audit independence: If manuscript status is unknown, how does audit scope affect cycles 83-84? Should audit focus on EARLY case studies (cycles 1-40, foundational) or RECENT case studies (cycles 71-82, variant methodology preparation)? Different focus → different effort. Recommend query manuscript status (what sections need support?) to scope audit.

Confidence trajectory post-Cycle 83: Current confidence 72% (up from 68% due to correction success). Projection assumes confidence 78%+ if retrieval works, 68% if bottleneck. But actual confidence Cycle 84 depends on EXECUTION RESULTS (not just plan). Define leading indicators: What measurements Cycle 83 would trigger confidence increase vs. decrease? E.g., 'retrieval success rate >90% → confidence +5%'?

Publication timeline visibility: Paper 1 (manuscript status unknown). Paper 2 (empirical search Cycles 80-85). Are they sequential (Paper 1 submitted Cycle 83, then Paper 2 after Cycle 85)? Or parallel (submit Paper 1 Cycle 83, submit Paper 2 Cycle 84)? Timeline affects resource allocation. Recommend clarify Paper 1/2 timeline Cycle 83 turn 250 alongside manuscript query.

Ephemeral layer robustness: Retrieval assumes facts remain in MEMORY.md + artifacts. But what if artifact is accidentally deleted? What if MEMORY.md line number shifts (artifact above it grows)? Index becomes invalid. Should Cycle 83 add integrity checking (verify index entries exist before retrieval attempt)? Or accept risk + manually repair index if corruption detected?
instructions_for_next:
**CYCLE 83→84 CRITICAL PATH (UPDATED post-turns 247-249):**

**COMPLETION CHECKPOINT (CYCLE 82-83 boundary, turn 249):**

Feedback artifact creation + MEMORY.md indexing COMPLETE. Data correction loop CLOSED. Pichay fault rate now persistent + linked. This clears blocking dependency from Cycle 82. NEXT cycles can proceed with retrieval architecture design.

Cycle 83-84 assumes:
- Pichay 2% fault rate (live deployment) is NOW UNDERSTOOD + DOCUMENTED (feedback_pichay_fault_rate.md)
- Retrieval is LOAD-BEARING (not optional)
- Autoresearch loop methodology is SOUND
- Sequencing option (Option 1 vs 2) is STILL TO BE CONFIRMED turn 250

**IMMEDIATE (Cycle 83, turns 250-260):**

(1) **CONFIRM SEQUENCING DECISION (TURN 250—GATE):**
   
   Cycle 82 instructions proposed two options:
   - Option 1 (RECOMMENDED): Cycles 82-83 = Architecture foundation (retrieval setup + Variant B baseline only). Cycles 84-85 = Empirical search (Variants A/C testing + convergence).
   - Option 2: Cycles 82-83 = Foundation + compressed variant testing (all three variants). Cycles 84-85 = Extended search + validation.
   
   DECISION was NOT EXPLICITLY RECORDED in turns 247-249.
   
   ACTION Cycle 83 turn 250: **Explicitly state which option was executed in Cycles 82 turns 244-246.** If user (turn 244) or context suggests Option 2, adjust Phase sequence below. If unclear, RECOMMEND Option 1 for methodological clarity.
   
   This is CRITICAL. Subsequent phases depend on choice.

(2) **PHASE A: RETRIEVAL PROTOCOL DESIGN (TURNS 250-252):**
   
   a) Create retrieval_protocol.md artifact:
      - Define query function: INPUT [concept_keyword, turn_number, optional_date_range] → OUTPUT [fact_text, source_artifact, retrieval_confidence_0to1]
      - Specify latency SLA: <200ms per request (or clarify what this means in token-budget terms)
      - Fallback logic: If retrieval fails, model continues without fact (measure as retrieval_success = FALSE)
      - Error handling: What if index is stale? (Verify entry exists before retrieval attempt)
      - Examples: 'Retrieve constraint from turn 78' should trigger query matching 'turn 78' + 'constraint' + return relevant lines from MEMORY.md or artifacts
      
   b) Create index_retrieval_map.md artifact:
      - Decide: Single artifact (index_retrieval_map.md) or integrated into MEMORY.md top section?
      - RECOMMEND: Separate artifact for clarity. Template format: [CYCLE_TURN_CONCEPT → source_artifact/line_number]
      - Examples:
        * 80_78_constraint_genealogy → design_decision_log.md:line_24
        * 81_feedback_pichay_fault_rate → feedback_pichay_fault_rate.md:full
        * 82_retrieval_reactive_protocol → retrieval_protocol.md:section_query_function
      - Scope: Index HIGH-LEVEL concepts first (design_decisions, limits_discovery, feedback, measurement_schemas). Expand to granular facts if retrieval success rate <80% (Cycle 83 turn 256 checkpoint).
      
   c) Define index maintenance process:
      - When: Each time new artifact created, update index within same turn (or batch updates at cycle end?)
      - Responsibility: Model maintains index during turn (lightweight add-to-index action)
      - Validation: Cycle-end check—does every indexed entry still exist? Flag if stale.
      
   d) Specify retrieval query execution:
      - Who executes queries? (Model makes request in reasoning → System (or external search) returns result)
      - Format: Model writes '[RETRIEVE: concept_keyword, turn_number]' in reasoning → Gets back [fact_text] to incorporate
      - Measurement: Log each retrieval attempt—[turn, query, result_found, latency_estimate, fact_used_in_reasoning]
      
   **Turn 252 Checkpoint: retrieval_protocol.md + index_retrieval_map.md created + indexed in MEMORY.md. Query function + index structure LOCKED IN for Phases B-D.**

(3) **PHASE B: BASELINE FAULT RATE DIAGNOSTIC (TURNS 252-254):**
   
   Goal: Measure fault rate for CURRENT monolithic tensor (Hamutay, no variant change) WITH retrieval available.
   
   Protocol:
   - Run 3-5 turns WITHOUT changing compression (use current 80/20 durable/ephemeral split from Cycle 80)
   - Retrieval ENABLED (but model may not need it if no facts are evicted)
   - Measurement: [turn, facts_in_tensor_at_start, facts_needed_during_turn, facts_found_in_tensor, facts_missing_requiring_retrieval, retrieval_requests, retrieval_success, fault_event_count]
   - Definition of FAULT: Model needs fact X (expressed as 'I need constraint from turn 78'), fact X is NOT in current tensor, must be retrieved. If retrieval succeeds, it's a HANDLED FAULT. If retrieval fails, it's an UNHANDLED FAULT (reasoning breaks down).
   - Hypothesis: Baseline fault rate ~1-3% (based on Pichay 2% live deployment, Hamutay slightly more aggressive)
   
   Create artifact: baseline_fault_rate_measurement.md
   - Results: [turn_1, turn_2, turn_3, turn_4, turn_5] with fault counts
   - Aggregate: Total faults / total turns = baseline_fault_rate_%
   - Analysis: Are faults clustered (certain turn types trigger more faults) or distributed? Does fatigue accumulate (fault rate increases over turns)?
   - Findings: Is baseline within expected 1-3%? Or higher (new risk)? Or lower (good news)?
   
   **Turn 254 Checkpoint: baseline_fault_rate_measurement.md created + analyzed. Empirical baseline established. This informs Variant A/C thresholds.**

(4) **PHASE C: RETRIEVAL END-TO-END TEST (TURNS 254-256):**
   
   Goal: Confirm retrieval mechanism works (index is accurate, queries are answered, latency is acceptable).
   
   Protocol:
   - Run 2-3 turns with retrieval ENABLED
   - Model explicitly requests facts (trigger retrieval when needed)
   - Log each retrieval: [turn, query_made, query_text, index_lookup_success, fact_returned, fact_relevance_0to1, latency_estimate_ms, fact_incorporated_in_reasoning]
   - Measurement: retrieval_success_rate = (facts_returned_correctly) / (retrieval_requests) × 100%
   - SLA validation: Is latency <200ms per request? (Or define latency baseline if wall-clock measure not available)
   - Debug: If success_rate <90%, investigate:
     * Index missing entries? (Expand index coverage)
     * Query semantics unclear? (Refine index structure from key-value to structured artifact names)
     * Retrieval confidence too strict? (Relax confidence threshold, accept lower-confidence matches)
   
   Create artifact: retrieval_test_results.md
   - Test turns: [turn_A, turn_B, turn_C] with full retrieval logs
   - Aggregate: retrieval_success_rate_%, retrieval_latency_avg_ms, false_positive_rate (returned fact not relevant), false_negative_rate (query failed but fact existed)
   - Decision: If success_rate >90% + latency acceptable → PASS. Proceed to Phase D.
            If success_rate 80-90% → MARGINAL. Debug one turn, reattempt.
            If success_rate <80% → FAIL. Escalate. May need index redesign (structured artifact names instead of key-value).
   
   **Turn 256 Checkpoint: retrieval mechanism validated. Gate 1 (retrieval working?) PASSED or ESCALATED.**

(5) **PHASE D: VARIANT B BASELINE TEST (TURNS 256-260):**
   
   Goal: Measure Variant B (current 80/20 durable/ephemeral) with retrieval enabled. Establish baseline for comparing A/C.
   
   Protocol:
   - Run 5-10 turns with Variant B (80/20 split, no change from Cycle 80) + retrieval ENABLED
   - Full measurement: [cycle, turn, variant, tensor_tokens, evicted_facts_count, retrieval_requests, retrieval_success_rate, retrieval_latency_avg_ms, working_set_tokens_added, total_context_tokens_used, reasoning_accuracy%, metric_reasoning_accuracy_per_tensor_token]
   - Comparison: How does Variant B with retrieval differ from Cycle 80 without retrieval?
     * Cycle 80: reasoning_accuracy% = X (estimated)
     * Cycle 83 Variant B: reasoning_accuracy% = Y, + retrieval_latency Z
     * Delta: Does retrieval improve reasoning (Y > X)? Or add overhead that doesn't offset (Y < X)? Latency trade-off?
   - Measurement schema: Use template from Cycle 82 (updated with retrieval columns). Create CSV: variant_b_baseline_measurement.csv
   
   Create artifact: variant_b_baseline_results.md
   - Results summary: reasoning_accuracy%, metric per tensor token, retrieval requests/latency
   - Comparison table: Cycle 80 (no retrieval) vs. Cycle 83 Variant B (with retrieval) on reasoning_quality
   - Confidence: Can we trust metric difference? (Is sample size large enough? Multiple runs to reduce noise?)
   - Implication: Is Variant B with retrieval better/same/worse than Cycle 80 baseline? If BETTER, retrieval + Variant B is validated. If WORSE, investigate (wrong metric? retrieval overhead too high?).
   
   **Turn 260 Checkpoint: Variant B baseline complete. Gate 2 (metric comparable to Cycle 80?) PASSED or INVESTIGATED.**

**DECISION GATE (TURN 260):**

If all four phases complete + no critical issues:
- Phase A ✓ (retrieval protocol designed + indexed)
- Phase B ✓ (baseline fault rate measured)
- Phase C ✓ (retrieval tested + working)
- Phase D ✓ (Variant B baseline measured + stable)
→ **PROCEED to Cycles 84-85: Full Variant A/B/C testing + convergence.**

If blockers detected (Phase C failed, Variant B metric unreliable, token budget exceeded):
→ **ESCALATE. Defer Variants A/C to Cycle 84. Focus Cycle 83 remaining turns (261+) on unblocking issue.**

**PARALLEL WORK (IF DUAL-PIPELINE CONFIRMED):**

If Cycle 83 manuscript status query returns 'on track' + turns available after Phase D:
- CASE STUDY AUDIT (turns 260-280, if tokens remain):
  * Review cycles 1-79 case studies
  * Add context metadata to each claim
  * Create case_study_audit_findings.md artifact
  * Goal: Paper 1 defensibility enhanced

If manuscript bottlenecked or turns exhausted:
- DEFER audit to Cycle 84. Single-pipeline (retrieval priority).

**MANUSCRIPT STATUS QUERY (STILL CRITICAL):**

Cycle 82 deferred this off-channel. **MUST be executed Cycle 83 turn 250-255.**

Query: Paper 1 completion %? Can submit Cycle 83? If bottlenecked, arxiv preprint instead?
Response determines: (1) Dual vs. single pipeline. (2) Paper 1/2 publication timeline. (3) Model resource allocation Cycles 83-85.

**CYCLE 83 MEMORY.MD ARTIFACTS (TO BE CREATED):**

1. retrieval_protocol.md (turns 250-252) — Query function, SLA, fallback logic
2. index_retrieval_map.md (turns 250-252) — Key-value index of MEMORY.md entries
3. baseline_fault_rate_measurement.md (turns 252-254) — Empirical baseline results
4. retrieval_test_results.md (turns 254-256) — Retrieval mechanism validation
5. variant_b_baseline_results.md (turns 256-260) — Variant B performance with retrieval
6. variant_b_baseline_measurement.csv (turns 256-260) — Full measurement table

OPTIONAL (if tokens available):
7. case_study_audit_findings.md (turns 260-280, parallel with audit)
8. data_integrity_standards.md (documents declared losses + feedback + preventive definitions)

Update MEMORY.md index after each artifact creation.

**CYCLE 83 CONFIDENCE CHECKPOINT:**

- START: 72% (correction success, retrieval risk)
- IF Phase A-D all pass: confidence increases to 78% (architecture foundation solid)
- IF Phase C fails: confidence drops to 65% (retrieval risk materialized, escalation needed)
- IF Variant B metric unreliable: confidence 70% (investigate metric definition)

Post-Cycle 83, update overall_truth/indeterminacy/falsity in tensor accordingly.

**CYCLES 84-85 PREVIEW (CONTINGENT ON CYCLE 83 SUCCESS):**

Cycle 84: Full Variant A (aggressive 60/40) / C (conservative 95/5) testing (5-10 turns each). Measure metric for A, B (already measured), C. Identify optimal range.

Cycle 85: Convergence + validation. Refine around optimal split (test neighboring variants). Run at scale. Prepare Paper 2 results.

Both contingent on:
- Retrieval mechanism working (Phase C ✓)
- Variant B baseline stable (Phase D ✓)
- Measurement schema reliable (turns 256+ validate metric consistency)

**CRITICAL SEQUENCING RULES CYCLES 83-85:**

(1) Do NOT start Variant A/C testing without retrieval in place. Otherwise failure mode is 'aggressive without retrieval' not 'compression limit.'
(2) Do NOT compare Variant A/B/C without baseline (Variant B) already measured. Otherwise cannot calibrate success.
(3) Do NOT publish results until drift rate <10% (confidence in data integrity). Pichay correction was 1 drift / 3 turns = 33% (acceptable for cycle, too high for publication).
(4) Do NOT defer manuscript status query past Cycle 83 turn 255. Delays decision on Paper 1/2 timeline.

**DISCIPLINE FOR CYCLES 83-85:**

Ongoing correction pattern: If drift detected (metric definition changes, assumption found wrong), CREATE feedback artifact immediately. Don't wait for cycle end. Feedback is REAL-TIME.

Ongoing preventive definitions: For each new metric/variable introduced Cycles 84-85, PRE-SPECIFY boundary conditions (what it means, when it applies, how it might drift). Document in definitions_cycle84.md artifact.

Ongoing declared losses: If tensor drops information, declare it. Silent loss is enemy of reproducibility.

**TOKEN BUDGET CYCLES 83:**

- Phase A (retrieval design): 8-10k tokens
- Phase B (baseline measurement): 6-8k tokens
- Phase C (retrieval test): 6-8k tokens
- Phase D (Variant B baseline): 12-15k tokens
- Artifacts + indexing: 4-6k tokens
- Manuscript query + audit (if dual-pipeline): 8-10k tokens

Total: 44-57k tokens (within estimated 50-60k cycle capacity, tight but feasible).

If exceeding cap by turn 260: DEFER audit to Cycle 84, prioritize retrieval + Variant B.

**SUCCESS CRITERIA CYCLE 83:**

✓ Feedback_pichay_fault_rate.md created + indexed (COMPLETE turn 249)
✓ Retrieval protocol designed + index created (target turn 252)
✓ Baseline fault rate measured (target turn 254)
✓ Retrieval mechanism tested + working (target turn 256)
✓ Variant B baseline measured + metric stable (target turn 260)
✓ Manuscript status queried (target turn 250-255)
✓ MEMORY.md artifacts created + indexed (ongoing)
✓ Confidence 78%+ (if phases pass) OR escalation triggered (if bottleneck detected)

If >6/8 achieved without critical escalation: Cycle 83 SUCCESSFUL. Proceed to Cycles 84-85.
If <6/8 OR critical escalation: Cycle 83 PARTIAL. Escalate blocker, reattempt in Cycle 84.
